summary: I was born in Somalia in 1985 and attended Shafii Secondary School there. My childhood memories are deeply rooted in Somalia, where I developed a passion for reading and learning. Unfortunately, due to the situation in Somalia, I couldn't continue my undergraduate education and had to move away. Somalia is where my educational journey and curiosity began.

After leaving Somalia because of the unrest, I moved to Qatar and lived there for a few years. Although I couldn't continue my formal education in Qatar, I kept my curiosity alive and learned a lot about the country and its surroundings. My time in Qatar was marked by a thirst for knowledge, even without access to school.

I moved to the United States in September 2014 with the goal of continuing my education. Living here has given me the chance to explore and learn more, especially in the Twin Cities area. The United States represents a new chapter in my life where I can pursue my passion for knowledge and education.

When I arrived in Minneapolis, Minnesota, I spent a lot of time alone, reading and asking questions about everything around me. Minneapolis became a place where I could dive into learning about local history and culture, often using Google to help me understand more. It’s part of the Twin Cities where I’ve grown my curiosity and knowledge since moving to the US.

Minnesota is where I settled after moving to the United States in 2014. I have spent a lot of time exploring its history, including learning about the Vikings and Native American heritage. Minnesota has been a rich source of knowledge for me, especially as I navigate my new life here with the help of Google.

The Twin Cities area, which includes Minneapolis and St. Paul, has been my home since I came to the US. I often use Google to understand the differences between places like Hennepin County and Ramsey County, or Minneapolis and St. Paul. This region has been central to my learning and adapting to life in America.

Google has been my best friend since my high school days in Somalia. It helped me satisfy my endless curiosity by providing quick and easy access to information. Throughout my journey—from Somalia to Qatar and now in the United States—Google has been my loyal companion, helping me learn about everything from history to local geography.

In Minnesota, I became fascinated with the history of the Vikings, which I often researched using Google. Learning about the Vikings is part of my broader interest in the history of the area, including Native American stories. The Vikings represent an important cultural and historical topic that I explored after moving to the Twin Cities.

I often found myself confused about the differences between Hennepin County and Minneapolis when I first arrived. Using Google, I learned that Hennepin County includes Minneapolis and is a key administrative region in Minnesota. Understanding this helped me better grasp the local geography and governance in my new home.

Like Hennepin County, I was curious about Ramsey County and how it relates to St. Paul. Google helped me understand that Ramsey County includes St. Paul and is another important part of the Twin Cities area. Learning about Ramsey County was part of my effort to understand the local community and geography after moving here.

St. Paul is part of the Twin Cities where I live, and I have spent time learning about it and its relationship to Ramsey County. Using Google, I explored the history and geography of St. Paul to better understand my surroundings. It’s an important city in my new life here in Minnesota.

I attended Shafii Secondary School in Somalia during my early education. It was there that my passion for reading and asking questions really took shape. My experiences at Shafii laid the foundation for my lifelong curiosity and love of learning.
